entries.q: What is Layrix exactly?
entries.a:
Layrix is a design system manager for Elementor. You define your design values (colors, fonts, spacing, shadows, radii) and classes centrally — Layrix syncs everything automatically to Elementor so the values are available there as global variables and classes.

The benefit: a change in the Layrix backend immediately affects all pages and elements that use the corresponding variable or class.

For a detailed explanation of the three layers (Layrix Settings, Elementor Variables, Global Classes), see the How does it work? page.
entries.keywords: layrix design system tokens elementor what is three layers architecture

entries.q: Where should I start?
entries.a:
Three ways:

  1. Run the wizard — guides you through all areas (Getting Started → Start wizard)
  2. Status page — all toggles at a glance
  3. Colors → Hero Grid — quickly generate a color palette and apply it

Important: Auto-Sync is enabled by default — once you change values and save, they go to Elementor automatically.
entries.keywords: first steps start beginning wizard getting started onboarding

entries.q: Which Elementor version is supported?
entries.a:
Layrix requires the Editor V4 (atomic widgets) for full functionality. The global variables registry and global classes registry — the ones Layrix syncs into — were introduced with Editor V4: variables in Elementor 3.31 (V4 Alpha), variables manager and global classes in 3.33 (V4 Beta).

What works where:

  • V4 atomic widgets (recommended, Elementor 3.33+): Variables appear in the style panel as tokens, global classes in the class editor, auto-classes are set directly in widget settings (visible as chip).
  • V3 classic widgets: No token picker in the editor. You can manually paste Layrix CSS classes into the „CSS Classes" field — they apply on the frontend because the CSS is output via wp_head. Auto-classes are appended as a CSS class on the element wrapper via the elementor/frontend/widget/before_render hook.

In short: with V3 widgets, Layrix tokens and class defaults are not pickable in the editor; they still apply on the frontend once the class is on the element.
entries.keywords: elementor version v3 v4 atomic widgets supported compatible variables global classes

entries.q: Do I need a license for Layrix?
entries.a: No. Layrix is a standalone plugin and does not require a license key. Updates are delivered directly from GitHub (when update checks are active, which is the default).
entries.keywords: license key paid free

entries.q: How do I back up my Layrix configuration?
entries.a:
Use the preset export/import feature (Sync page → Actions → Export preset). You receive a JSON file with all settings (colors, spacing, class defaults, auto-class toggles, etc.).

On another site, choose „Import preset" and select the JSON — everything is applied.
entries.keywords: export import backup save preset migration json configuration

entries.q: What does the Theme Style Importer pull from Elementor?
entries.a:
When you set up Layrix on an existing Elementor site, you can pull existing values from the active Elementor kit — saves manual typing.

What gets imported (when set in the kit):

  • System colors: primary, secondary, accent, text → Layrix colors
  • Custom colors → additional Layrix color slots
  • Body text color + background → base colors
  • Link color
  • Body font, size, weight, line-height
  • Heading font
  • Container width, content width

UI: button „Import from Elementor" on the Colors page → modal with preview per field + checkbox per row. You pick what to adopt, everything else stays untouched.
entries.keywords: theme style importer elementor kit import existing migration

entries.q: What is the difference between built-in and custom presets?
entries.a:
Built-in presets are bundled style templates (e.g. „Editorial Bold", „Lush Botanical"). Clicking one overwrites colors + radii + spacings + fonts with the template. Useful for quick starts or inspiration.

Custom presets are your own snapshots: current style → save → reuse later or import on another site. Stored in the DB, exportable/importable as JSON.
entries.keywords: preset built-in template custom own difference style presets

entries.q: How do I save my own preset?
entries.a:
Set up the current style as desired → in the preset selector there is a button „Save current style as preset". Pick a name → done.

Included in the export: all colors (incl. shades and dark-mode), radii, spacing, shadows, fonts, type scale, line-heights, letter-spacing, class defaults and auto-class toggles.

Not included: plugin UI settings (admin font size etc.), update toggle, custom notes.
entries.keywords: preset save own custom snapshot template style

entries.q: What is the difference between Website and Plugin tab in Settings?
entries.a:
Inside Settings there are two icon tabs:

  • ⌂ Website — what affects your live site: root font size, container width, content width, base colors, body font, font sizes, browser margin reset, focus outline.
  • ⚙ Plugin — what affects Layrix itself: auto-sync toggles, auto-class toggles, class defaults, GitHub updates, Layrix admin UI (font and font sizes of the interface).

Rule of thumb: „Does it affect the frontend output?" → Website. „Only the Layrix admin?" → Plugin.
entries.keywords: settings website plugin tab difference frontend admin sub-tab

entries.q: Do I need to sync manually?
entries.a:
No, when Auto-Sync is on (which is the default). Then sync runs automatically in the background after every save.

You only need to sync manually when:

  • You disabled Auto-Sync
  • You modified Layrix classes inside Elementor and want to push Layrix defaults back
entries.keywords: sync synchronize auto-sync manual automatic

entries.q: Where do I configure spacing?
entries.a:
Sidebar → Design Values → Spacing. You define the base spacing values (min/max for fluid clamp) and the scale (xs, s, m, l, xl, 2xl, 3xl).

Layrix generates the --ecf-space-* variables and syncs them to Elementor.
entries.keywords: spacing padding margin space token

entries.q: Where do I set colors?
entries.a:
Sidebar → Design Values → Colors. You have:

  • Hero Grid on top — harmony generator and live picker
  • Palette below — all active color values (primary, secondary, accent, surface, text, plus your own)

Click any card to open the native color picker. Auto-Save writes the value back immediately.
entries.keywords: colors color palette primary secondary accent

entries.q: How do I change the global font size?
entries.a:
There are two distinct font sizes:

  • Body text size (paragraph text): Settings → Website → Typography → Body Text Size
  • Heading sizes (h1–h5): Settings → Plugin → Class Defaults → Headings → ecf-heading-1..5 → font-size

Since 0.5.7 the body text size accepts px / rem / em / % / vw with auto-conversion when switching units.
entries.keywords: font-size body text typography heading

entries.q: Why can I not see shadows in the preview?
entries.a:
Since 0.5.7 the shadow preview renders on a light background (white square on light gray) — previously the background was dark, making dark shadows invisible.

If the preview still looks off: hard reload (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R) — likely a cached CSS file.
entries.keywords: shadow box-shadow preview invisible

entries.q: What are radius values?
entries.a:
Radius values control how round the corners of elements are. Instead of hardcoding border-radius: 8px everywhere, you define a scale (xs, s, m, l, xl, full) and reference it as --ecf-radius-m.

Layrix supports fluid clamp: a min and max value per token that interpolates between mobile and desktop.
entries.keywords: radius border corners round scale xs s m l xl full clamp

entries.q: What does min/max mean for spacing?
entries.a:
Layrix uses fluid clamp for spacing: each spacing value has a min (small screens) and a max (large screens). CSS interpolates between via clamp().

Benefit: spacing feels tighter on mobile and more generous on desktop without requiring breakpoints.
entries.keywords: min max spacing fluid clamp responsive viewport breakpoint

entries.q: What is the type scale?
entries.a:
The type scale is the scale of font sizes for headings and text. Layrix generates it from two values: base font size and ratio (e.g. 1.25 = Major Third).

This produces 15 steps from --ecf-text-5xs to --ecf-text-7xl. Heading classes use the upper half: ecf-heading-1 = text-4xl, ecf-heading-2 = text-3xl, ecf-heading-3 = text-2xl, ecf-heading-4 = text-xl, ecf-heading-5 = text-l. (There is intentionally no ecf-heading-6.)
entries.keywords: type-scale typography ratio font-sizes heading text

entries.q: How do I create custom variables?
entries.a:
Sidebar → Design Values → Variables. Define any custom CSS variable (e.g. --ecf-anim-fast: 200ms or --ecf-z-modal: 1000).

These are generated alongside the standard Layrix design values and synced together.
entries.keywords: custom variables css-variable z-index animation duration

entries.q: What format do shadow values use?
entries.a:
Standard CSS box-shadow syntax: x-offset y-offset blur spread color. Examples:

  • Outer: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)
  • Inner: inset 0 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)

Multiple shadows comma-separated: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.07), 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,.04).
entries.keywords: shadow format box-shadow syntax inner inset outer rgba

entries.q: What is the Hero Grid on the Colors page?
entries.a:
The Hero Grid is Layrix' harmony generator: a sandbox at the top of the Colors page with 5 color slots (primary, secondary, accent, surface, text), where you can experiment with palettes without committing the values immediately.

Workflow: play around → lock the slots you want to keep → „Shuffle" regenerates the unlocked ones → when you are happy, click „Apply to palette". Only then are the values written to your Layrix settings and synced.
entries.keywords: hero grid harmony generator colors palette

entries.q: What do Complementary, Analogous, Triadic mean in the harmony generator?
entries.a:
These are classic color harmony schemes from color theory. Layrix offers six modes:

  • Complementary — opposite colors on the wheel (max contrast)
  • Analogous — neighboring colors (calm, harmonious)
  • Triadic — 3 colors evenly spaced (balanced, vibrant)
  • Split — base + the two neighbors of the complementary (softer contrast)
  • Tetradic — 4 colors in two complementary pairs (versatile, tricky)
  • Monochromatic — one color in different lightness/saturation

With no slot locked, the entire scale regenerates; with locked slots, only free ones are re-rolled — fitting the scheme.
entries.keywords: harmony modes complementary analogous triadic split tetradic monochromatic color theory wheel

entries.q: What are the lock icons in the Hero Grid for?
entries.a:
The lock icon on a slot locks that color — the next „Shuffle" or scheme change leaves it untouched. Unlocked slots are recomputed based on the locked ones and the current harmony mode.

Typical workflow: lock your brand color (e.g. primary) → cycle through the others until they fit → apply.
entries.keywords: lock icon slot color keep hero grid

entries.q: „Generate from image" — how does it work?
entries.a:
Click the image icon in the Hero Grid → choose an image → Layrix extracts the 5 dominant colors client-side (browser, no server upload) and places them in the slots.

Useful when you have a logo, mood board, or photo as inspiration and want to derive a consistent palette from it.
entries.keywords: image generate extract upload palette photo logo

entries.q: What are leading and tracking?
entries.a:
Two typography terms:

  • Leading = line-height. Layrix manages it as --ecf-leading-* tokens (e.g. tight, snug, normal, relaxed, loose). Tab: Typography → Line Heights.
  • Tracking = letter-spacing. Managed as --ecf-tracking-* tokens. Tab: Typography → Letter Spacing. em units recommended (relative to font size).
entries.keywords: leading tracking line-height letter-spacing typography

entries.q: Can I store a dark-mode value for a color?
entries.a:
Yes. The edit panel of every color has a collapsible section „🌙 Dark Mode variant". Activate the „Dark mode value active" checkbox and set the hex for dark mode.

Layrix then generates CSS that swaps the value via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark). The site reacts automatically to the user's OS theme.
entries.keywords: dark mode color variant prefers-color-scheme

entries.q: What do rem, em, vw mean for body text size?
entries.a:
Layrix supports several CSS units:

  • px — fixed pixel size
  • rem — relative to root font size
  • em — relative to parent element
  • % — percent of parent
  • vw — percent of viewport width

Note on rem math: Layrix offers two root modes (Settings → Website → Root Font Size): 62.5% (10px base, so 1rem = 10px) or 100% (16px base, so 1rem = 16px). Other values fall back to 100%.

Switching units, Layrix auto-converts — 18px becomes 1.8rem (at 62.5% root) or 1.125rem (at 100% root).
entries.keywords: rem em vw px unit conversion font-size

entries.q: What is the ecf-button class?
entries.a:
ecf-button is the Layrix base class for buttons. It contains geometry and typography:

  • Padding (block + inline from spacing values)
  • Border-radius (from radius value)
  • Font size (from type scale)

Deliberately not included: background-color, color, hover. Those come from the user or Elementor default. So Layrix never overrides your custom colors.
entries.keywords: ecf-button button class base components

entries.q: What are class defaults?
entries.a:
Class defaults are the standard values a Layrix class carries — e.g. how much padding a button has, what font size a heading uses, what max-width a container uses.

Layrix manages them centrally so you do not have to touch every single element in Elementor. One change in the backend → every element using that class picks up the new value.

Defaults reference no fixed pixel values but design-value variables (e.g. ecf-space-m) — keeping the system consistent: change the spacing value and every class follows.
entries.keywords: class-defaults what are defaults design-values settings explanation

entries.q: Where do I configure class defaults (padding, radius, font size)?
entries.a:
Settings → Plugin → Class Defaults. All Layrix classes are grouped by category as collapsible accordions.

Per property you pick a design value via dropdown. On the next sync Layrix writes the value into the Elementor global class — every element using that class gets the new value automatically.
entries.keywords: class-defaults defaults padding radius font-size token settings where

entries.q: Which classes can I configure and which properties?
entries.a:
Currently these classes have configurable defaults:

  • Headings ecf-heading-1 … ecf-heading-5 → font-size (from type scale)
  • Components ecf-button → padding-block, padding-inline, border-radius, font-size
  • Sections ecf-layrix-section → padding-block, padding-inline
  • Layout ecf-container-boxed → max-width

Other Layrix classes (e.g. ecf-card, ecf-section) currently have no defaults — they are pure identity classes you style as needed in Elementor's class editor.
entries.keywords: which classes defaults configure properties heading button section container list overview

entries.q: What happens when I change a class default?
entries.a:
On save in Layrix:

  1. The value is written to Layrix settings
  2. With Auto-Sync on (default), the Elementor global class is updated immediately
  3. Every element using that class — across 1 or 100 pages — shows the new value

Important: Per-element customizations (values you set on a specific button in the editor) remain untouched. They have higher specificity and beat the class default. Only elements that did not set anything custom adopt the new default.
entries.keywords: default change what happens effect sync elementor specificity custom-values

entries.q: Why can I not set button color in class defaults?
entries.a:
Deliberate decision since 0.6.1.1: ecf-button manages only geometry and typography (padding, radius, font size) — no colors (background-color, color, hover).

Reasons:

  • Atomic widgets in Elementor v4 do not store style props in container.settings but in a separate styles model. Layrix could not reliably write colors there — the picker in the inspector often lied.
  • If Layrix writes default colors to the global class, those would block your user-custom colors on the element (specificity issue).

Solution: set colors per element directly in the Elementor editor. Layrix handles geometry consistency, you handle the color language.
entries.keywords: button color background hover not configurable why ecf-button class-defaults

entries.q: What does the double underscore in ecf-card__title mean?
entries.a:
Layrix uses BEM naming (Block Element Modifier):

  • ecf-card is the block (parent element)
  • ecf-card__title is an element inside the block (double underscore)
  • ecf-button--primary is a variant (double dash)

Benefit: hierarchical relationships are immediately readable from the class name.
entries.keywords: bem naming convention block element modifier underscore card title

entries.q: Utility classes vs. starter classes — what is the difference?
entries.a:
Starter classes are component-like: ecf-card, ecf-button, ecf-section. They have an identity character (what is this thing?) and carry Layrix defaults for padding, radius, etc.

Utility classes are atomic single-purpose: ecf-shadow-l or ecf-text-2xl. They do exactly one thing and combine.
entries.keywords: utility starter difference classes components atomic single-purpose

entries.q: How do I create a custom Layrix class?
entries.a:
Class Selection page → bottom „Custom classes" section → plus button. Specify name, optional description, category.

Custom classes are synced. You write styles for them in Elementor (class editor) or via custom CSS.
entries.keywords: custom class create new add

entries.q: What is the Application tab?
entries.a:
Sidebar → Classes → Application. Card overview of all Layrix classes per category (Headings, Sections, Components, Layout). Clicking a card copies the class name to clipboard.

Workflow: click card → in Elementor select element → CSS classes field → paste.
entries.keywords: application cookbook tab cards copy clipboard overview classes

entries.q: What are the shadow utility classes (ecf-shadow-xs, ecf-shadow-l, …)?
entries.a:
For every shadow token tier, Layrix generates a matching utility class:

  • ecf-shadow-xs through ecf-shadow-xl (outer shadows)
  • ecf-shadow-inner (inner shadow)

Classes are atomic — one class = one shadow. Activate them under Class Selection → Utility and they get synced as global Elementor classes.

Benefit over inline-style: central control. Change the shadow token value and every element with ecf-shadow-l updates automatically.
entries.keywords: shadow utility classes ecf-shadow xs s m l xl inner

entries.q: How do I use Layrix classes inside Elementor?
entries.a:
Three ways:

  1. Auto-Classes: when active, newly inserted widgets receive their class automatically (h1–h5 → ecf-heading-N, button → ecf-button, etc.)
  2. Class field: select element in Elementor → CSS classes field → paste class name
  3. Cookbook: Layrix page „Application" → click card → class is copied → paste in Elementor
entries.keywords: classes use elementor editor css-classes application

entries.q: What happens during a sync?
entries.a:
Layrix writes two things to Elementor's database:

  • Variables (colors, fonts, spacing, radii, shadows) → Elementor's global-variables registry. Visible in the style panel as tokens.
  • Classes (ecf-button, ecf-heading-N, …) → Elementor's global-classes registry. Visible in the class editor.

User customizations on classes (e.g. background-color) remain untouched since 0.6.1.2 — Layrix merges variant-per-meta instead of replacing the whole list.
entries.keywords: sync synchronization what happens elementor variables classes

entries.q: Auto-Sync vs. manual sync — what is the difference?
entries.a:
Auto-Sync (on by default): after every save in Layrix, sync runs automatically in the background. No clicks required.

Manual sync: click „Sync with Elementor" on the Sync tab. Before syncing, Layrix checks whether you modified Layrix classes inside Elementor — if so, a conflict modal opens with per-conflict choice.
entries.keywords: auto-sync manual difference automatic

entries.q: What is the conflict modal during sync?
entries.a:
If you adjusted values on a Layrix class itself inside Elementor (e.g. set ecf-button padding-inline to a different value), Layrix shows a conflict modal during manual sync listing all divergences.

Per conflict you choose:

  • Adopt Elementor (default): Layrix backend is updated to match the Elementor value
  • Force Layrix: backend value overrides Elementor value
entries.keywords: conflict modal sync divergence elementor adopt force

entries.q: Why does the Status page show „Not yet synced"?
entries.a:
This happens when neither variables nor classes have arrived in Elementor's registries yet. Common causes:

  • Fresh install and no save has happened yet
  • Auto-Sync is off and you never synced manually
  • Elementor was inactive during sync (e.g. plugin temporarily disabled)

Just click „Sync now" — the warning disappears immediately on success.
entries.keywords: warning not synced status page banner

entries.q: Sync fails — what do I do?
entries.a:
Common causes:

  • Elementor limit reached: Elementor enforces a hard cap on global variables and classes (currently 100 per registry since Elementor 3.33; previously 50). This limit applies to all plans — Free and Pro are identical. The Sync page shows current usage at the top right. Solution: remove unused Layrix tokens or classes — a Pro upgrade does not raise this limit.
  • Elementor inactive: Layrix needs Elementor to sync. Activate the plugin.
  • Cache conflict: some caching plugins block REST requests. Pause caching plugin and re-sync.
entries.keywords: sync error fail limit cache rest 500

entries.q: What is the CSS drawer on the Sync page?
entries.a:
On the Sync page there is a collapsible CSS drawer with the complete frontend CSS that Layrix generates (all --ecf-* variables, class definitions, reset rules).

You can:

  • Inspect — see what Layrix actually serves to your site
  • Copy to clipboard
  • Download as layrix-generated.css

Useful when you want to disable Layrix later (embed CSS into the theme) or want to use the CSS in a build step.
entries.keywords: css drawer export download generate standalone offline copy

entries.q: My custom values on a Layrix class disappeared!
entries.a:
Known issue in versions before 0.6.1.2 — Layrix replaced the entire variants array on a class during sync instead of merging only its own properties. Custom values (background, :hover, etc.) were lost.

Fixed in 0.6.1.2: variants are now merged per (breakpoint+state). Layrix only overrides its own prop keys (padding, font-size, …); all other props remain.

If you are on 0.6.1.2: set your custom values once again, they will be preserved on future syncs.
entries.keywords: class disappeared lost reset default background hover custom

entries.q: Which auto-classes are active after installation?
entries.a:
By default (fresh install) ALL auto-classes are active:

  • Master toggle: on
  • Headings (h1–h5 → ecf-heading-N)
  • Buttons (→ ecf-button)
  • Text-Links (→ ecf-text-link)
  • Forms (→ ecf-form)

Existing installations keep their toggle states — Layrix does not override unprompted.
entries.keywords: default standard auto-classes active installation new

entries.q: How do I notice when auto-classes are accidentally off?
entries.a:
Layrix automatically shows a warning banner at the top of every Layrix page when the master toggle or any sub-toggle (Headings/Buttons/Text-Links/Forms) is disabled:

  • Yellow — master is on but individual sub-toggles are off (with list of which)
  • Red — master is off, no auto-classes work

The „Activate all" button in the banner re-enables every auto-class toggle in one click. The banner disappears immediately and auto-sync runs in the background.
entries.keywords: warning banner auto-classes off disabled notice display error

entries.q: Do auto-classes apply to existing elements?
entries.a:
No, only to newly inserted ones. Buttons/headings you built before activation do not receive the class automatically — you have to add them manually or re-insert the element.

Reason: Layrix does not touch existing Elementor data unprompted, otherwise users would lose control of what changes.
entries.keywords: existing elements auto-classes retroactive

entries.q: How do I turn auto-classes off?
entries.a:
Status page → Auto-Classes for new widgets → Master off. That disables all auto-class sub-toggles at once.

Or individually: just toggle „Headings" / „Buttons" / „Text-Links" / „Forms".
entries.keywords: auto-classes off disable toggle

entries.q: What is the Layrix Section widget in Elementor?
entries.a:
An atomic widget for Elementor v4 that inserts a ready-made Section + Inner Container structure:

  • Outer div automatically gets ecf-layrix-section (padding-block + padding-inline from Layrix spacing values)
  • Inner div automatically gets ecf-container-boxed (max-width from the container-boxed value)

Drag&Drop one widget instead of two nested flexbox containers.
entries.keywords: layrix section widget atomic v4 container boxed inner

entries.q: How do I update Layrix?
entries.a:
When GitHub update checks are active (default on), new versions appear automatically in the WordPress plugin list with an „Update available" button.

Update checks are triggered by:

  • WP-Cron in the background (default 12-hour interval)
  • Visiting the WP Plugins page (transient refresh)
  • Manually via „Check now" on the Layrix plugin update page

Server-side requirement: the GitHub release must have a ZIP asset (all releases since 0.5.4 do).
entries.keywords: update version github release latest

entries.q: How do I disable update checks?
entries.a:
Status page → Plugin Maintenance → GitHub Update Checks off. Layrix then no longer checks for new versions.

You will then have to install updates manually (download ZIP from GitHub → upload as plugin in WP).
entries.keywords: update check disable github checks

entries.q: Where can I see what changed in an update?
entries.a:
Three sources:

  • In the WP plugin directory: click „View details" on the Layrix plugin
  • On GitHub: github.com/alexus-online/layrix/releases
  • In the repo: CHANGELOG.md
entries.keywords: changelog version history release notes what changed

entries.q: Update check fails — what do I do?
entries.a:
Common error message: „GitHub release ZIP for version X.Y.Z is missing."

Means: the master branch advertises a version for which no published release with a ZIP exists. Solution: wait until the release is published or clear the update cache („Check now").
entries.keywords: update error fail zip github

entries.q: Layrix classes are not showing up in Elementor
entries.a:
Checks:

  1. Did you sync? — Status page top. If „⚠ Not yet synced" → click „Sync now".
  2. Auto-Sync on? — Status page → Sync section → check toggle.
  3. Class active? — Class Selection page → is the class checked?
  4. Cache? — reload the Elementor editor (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R), regenerate Elementor cache if needed.
entries.keywords: class not showing missing elementor empty sync not working

entries.q: My button suddenly has the wrong color
entries.a:
Known issue with Elementor v4 atomic buttons: the picker sometimes shows Elementor's default #375EFB while the page renders a different color. This is cosmetic — the page itself is correct.

If the page rendering is wrong: check whether you set custom colors on the element (not on the class). Element style beats class style. Since 0.6.1.2 element-level custom colors remain untouched by sync.
entries.keywords: button color wrong background blue default 375efb

entries.q: Layrix styles do not apply on the frontend
entries.a:
Checks:

  1. Cache: clear browser cache (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R) and flush caching plugins (WP Rocket, W3TC, etc.).
  2. Class assigned? — inspect the frontend, is the Layrix class on the element?
  3. CSS output on? — Layrix outputs CSS via wp_head. Check the page source for a style block with --ecf-* variables.
  4. Theme override: some themes override with !important. Browser DevTools show which rule wins.
entries.keywords: styles not applying frontend css cache theme override

entries.q: I cannot find a specific setting
entries.a:
Look at the Status page first — all main toggles are gathered there. If the setting is not there:

  • Design Values (colors, spacing, etc.) → left sidebar under „Design Values"
  • Class defaults (padding, radius of a class) → Settings → Plugin → Class Defaults
  • UI tweaks (admin font size, etc.) → Settings → Plugin → General

Tip: use the plugin search (bottom left ⌘K) to jump straight to a setting.
entries.keywords: cannot find setting search where is

entries.q: Layrix conflicts with another plugin
entries.a:
Common conflict sources:

  • Other design-system plugins (e.g. Bricks theme variable manager) — can write to the same Elementor classes. Disable one or change the class-name prefix.
  • Custom-CSS plugins with !important cascades — override Layrix variables. Check specificity.
  • Caching plugins blocking REST requests — exclude REST paths /wp-json/ecf-framework/* from cache.
entries.keywords: conflict plugin other incompatible cache rest

entries.q: How do I disable Layrix cleanly without breaking the site?
entries.a:
Layrix generates the frontend CSS dynamically. Without the plugin, all Layrix variables and class values disappear.

Clean exit:

  1. Sync page → CSS drawer → download CSS
  2. Embed CSS into your theme (child-theme style.css or Customizer)
  3. Disable Layrix — classes in HTML remain, CSS is now served from theme
entries.keywords: disable remove turn off clean break css export
